title: Girls Racing Fast Cars to Change Formula 1
summary: This article is about girls who love to race cars and want to be part of Formula 1, a big racing sport. Rachel Robertson is an 18-year-old from England who drives fast race cars in a special championship called F1 Academy, which is only for women. She started racing go-karts when she was 14 and often raced against boys who didn't think girls should race. They would try to push her off the track or not talk to her when she won. Another driver, Esmee Kosterman from the Netherlands, also faced boys who said racing wasn't for girls. Both girls are working hard to show that girls can be great racers and hope to race in Formula 1 one day. They are breaking barriers in a sport that has mostly been for men and rich people.

a racing championship only for women
small, fast cars used for beginner racing
things that make it hard for someone to do something

What did boys sometimes do when Rachel raced against them?
What did boys tell Esmee about racing?
What is the main goal of Rachel and Esmee?

background_read: Formula 1 is a famous car racing sport where drivers compete in fast cars around the world. For a long time, most drivers have been men, and it can be expensive to join. Some people think girls shouldn't race, but programs like F1 Academy help girls learn and show they can be fast drivers too. This article tells the story of two girls who are working hard to change this and make racing more inclusive for everyone.
Article_Structure: WHO: Rachel Robertson and Esmee Kosterman, teenage girls who race cars
WHAT: They are breaking into motorsport by racing in F1 Academy and facing challenges from boys who don't think girls should race
WHERE: England and the Netherlands, with racing in events like F1 Academy
WHY: To show that girls can be successful in Formula 1 and overcome barriers in a male-dominated sport

perspectives.description: Girls like Rachel and Esmee are working hard to prove they belong in motorsport and can compete at high levels.
